'''Shannon Jay McDonnell''' (born 5 August 1987) is a former [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who last played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]]. He had previously played for [[National Rugby League]] clubs the [[Wests Tigers]] and the [[Newcastle Knights]], and in the [[Super League]] for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] and [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]]. He primarily played as a {{rlp|fb}} and as a {{rlp|wg}}.

==Background==
McDonnell was born in [[Camden, New South Wales]], Australia. McDonnell is of Irish descent and attended [[Mount Carmel High School (Varroville)|Mount Carmel High School]] and [[Westfields Sports High School]], while playing his junior football for [[Western Suburbs Magpies#District Junior Competition|All Saints Liverpool]]. He was then signed by the [[Wests Tigers]]. He is the son of Wests Tigers Recruitment Manager Warren McDonnell.

==Playing career==
===Early career===
In 2004, McDonnell played for the [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] Under-17s team. The next season, he played for the New South Wales Under-19s team<ref name="smh 2005">{{cite news|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|title=Teenager waits in the wins for Richards|author=Brad Walter and Greg Prichard
|url=http://www.smh.com.au/news/league/teenager-waits-in-the-wins-for-richards/2005/09/29/1127804612484.html|accessdate=9 January 2011|date=30 September 2005}}</ref> and made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] début for the [[Wests Tigers]] against the [[Penrith Panthers]] in Round 26 of the [[2005 NRL season]].

===Wests Tigers===
Having played one game for Tigers in 2005, McDonnell was named as the probable replacement for winger [[Pat Richards]], if he was unable to overcome an ankle injury, to take his place in the [[2005 NRL Grand Final]] team.<ref name="smh 2005" />

In 2006, McDonnell played on the {{rlp|wg}} in the Tigers' 30–10 loss to [[Super League]] champions [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[2006 World Club Challenge]], and also played for the New South Wales Under-19s team and [[Junior Kangaroos]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thekennel.net.au/forum/showthread.php?t=24129|title=Junior kangaroos named|work=The Kennel |publisher=Thekennel.net.au|accessdate=6 January 2015}}</ref>
[[File:SHANNON MCDONNELL .jpg|thumb|right|McDonnell playing for the [[Western Suburbs Magpies]] in 2008]]

In 2008, McDonnell made himself available for [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]]'s [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]] campaign.<ref>Collins, Ben: "Kelly eyes Cup sitr by Irish", pages 18-19. Rugby League World, April 2008</ref><ref name="White Eyes a Green Jersey">{{cite web|url=http://www.rlwc08.com/news/news/archive/2008/07/30/white-eyes-a-green-jersey.aspx|title=White Eyes a Green Jersey|publisher=RLWC08.com|date=2008-07-21|accessdate=2008-07-30}}{{dead link|date=December 2016|bot=InternetArchiveBot|fix-attempted=yes}}</ref> Although named in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#Pool C|Ireland training squad]] for the World Cup,<ref name="Ireland Name World Cup 40 Man Training Squad">{{cite web|url=http://www.rli.ie/story.php?id=53|title=Ireland Name World Cup 40 Man Training Squad|publisher=Rugby League Ireland|date=2008-08-01|accessdate=2008-08-02|archive-date=9 October 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171009095326/http://www.rli.ie/story.php?id=53|url-status=dead}}</ref> he was forced to withdraw through injury.<ref name="Ireland suffer White injury blow">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7657596.stm|title=Ireland suffer White injury blow|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-09|access-date=2008-10-10}}</ref>

===Newcastle Knights===
On 7 May 2009, McDonnell joined the [[Newcastle Knights]] mid-season for the remainder of the year, after being released from his Tigers contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/breaking-news/mcdonnell-quits-tigers-for-knights/story-e6frf33c-1225710508152|title=Shannon McDonnell quits Wests Tigers for Newcastle|publisher=Foxsports.com.au|accessdate=6 January 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,25442694-5018866,00.html|title=Shannon McDonnell quits Wests Tigers for Newcastle|publisher=BBC|date=7 May 2009|accessdate=7 May 2009}}</ref>

At the conclusion of the [[2011 NRL season]], McDonnell joined Hull KR in the English Super League.<ref>{{cite web|last=Keeble|first=Brett|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/475702/knights-in-no-hurry-to-go-separate-ways/|title=Knights in no hurry to go separate ways|work=The Newcastle Herald|date=2011-09-06|access-date=2012-05-24}}</ref>

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
At the beginning of 2012, McDonnell joined [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]] on a 1-year contract, following his former Knights assistant coach [[Craig Sandercock]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/mcdonnell-signs-esl-side-hull-kr-4111|title=McDonnell signs with ESL side Hull KR|work=NRL - Zero Tackle|date=28 September 2011 |accessdate=6 January 2015}}</ref>

In June 2012, McDonnell played for the [[Exiles rugby league team|Exiles]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.hullkr.co.uk/on-the-pitch/news/article/mcdonnell-called-into-exiles-team|title=McDonnell called into Exiles team|publisher=Hullkr.co.uk|accessdate=6 January 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150106151501/http://www.hullkr.co.uk/on-the-pitch/news/article/mcdonnell-called-into-exiles-team|archive-date=6 January 2015|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===Hull F.C.===
On 26 October 2012, McDonnell signed a 2-year contract with Hull KR's cross city rival team in [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]], starting in 2013.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/19978627|title=Hull FC sign full-back Shannon McDonnell after Hull KR exit|date=26 October 2012 |publisher=BBC Sport|accessdate=6 January 2015}}</ref> In his first year at the club, he helped the team to the [[2013 Challenge Cup]] Final against the [[Wigan Warriors]] where they were defeated 16–0.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12210/8846741/tetleys-challenge-cup-hull-fc-full-back-shannon-mcdonnell-battling-to-be-fit-for-the-final|title=Tetley's Challenge Cup: Hull FC full-back Shannon McDonnell battling to be fit for the final|publisher=Sky Sports|accessdate=6 January 2015}}</ref>

===St Helens===
On 3 July 2014, McDonnell joined [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] mid-season for the remainder of the year, after resigning from his Hull F.C. contract.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/28151548|title=Shannon McDonnell: St Helens sign former Hull FC full-back|date=3 July 2014 |publisher=BBC Sport|accessdate=6 January 2015}}</ref>

In October and November 2014, McDonnell played for Ireland in the [[2014 European Cup (rugby league)|2014 European Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12040/9511047/rugby-league-shannon-mcdonnell-in-irelands-european-championship-squad|title=Rugby League: Shannon McDonnell in Ireland's European Championship squad|work=Sky Sports |accessdate=6 January 2015}}</ref> He scored two tries on début as Ireland beat [[France national rugby league team|France]] 22–12 in their European Cup opener.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_report/0,19936,11070_3784566,00.html|title=Match Report - Ireland v France - 18th October 2014 - Sky Sports - Rugby League News|publisher=Sky Sports|accessdate=6 January 2015|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150208160423/http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_report/0,19936,11070_3784566,00.html|archivedate=8 February 2015}}</ref>

On 5 January 2015, McDonnell signed a train and trial contract to return to his first club [[Wests Tigers]], in the hope of impressing for a second-tier contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.weststigers.com.au/news/2015/01/05/wests_tigers_add_dep.html|title=Wests Tigers add depth to pre-season squad|date=5 January 2015 |publisher=Weststigers.com.au|accessdate=6 January 2015}}</ref>

On 30 April 2015, McDonnell rejoined [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] effective immediately on a 2-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.sbs.com.au/news/article/2015/04/30/mcdonnel-rejoins-super-leagues-st-helens|title=McDonnel rejoins Super League's St Helens | SBS News|publisher=Sbs.com.au|date=2015-04-30|accessdate=2016-09-27}}</ref>

In 2016, he was called up to the Ireland squad for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup qualification – Europe#Group B|2017 Rugby League World Cup European Pool B qualifiers]].<ref name="Warrington Wolves trio named in Ireland squad for World Cup qualifiers">{{cite web|url=http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/14763089.Warrington_Wolves_trio_named_in_Ireland_squad_for_World_Cup_qualifiers|title=Warrington Wolves trio named in Ireland squad for World Cup qualifiers|publisher=Warrington Guardian|date=24 September 2016|accessdate=26 September 2016}}</ref>

===Camden Rams===
McDonnell played for the [[Camden Rams]] in 2017.<ref name="Rugby League World Cup: Ireland name squad"/>

In October 2017, Shannon was called up to the Ireland squad for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Rugby League World Cup: Ireland name squad">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/41536076|title=Rugby League World Cup: Ireland name squad|date=7 October 2017|publisher=BBC}}</ref>
